stories.headline: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

stories.headline: Bayer wins crucial US Supreme Court ruling over Roundup weedkiller
stories.summary: Justices overturn verdict that became basis for billions of dollars in claims over warnings of alleged cancer risk

stories.headline: Trump Seeks $88 Billion for Iran War
stories.summary: President Donald Trump asked Congress to pass a supplemental spending package to backfill the cost of the war in Iran and respond to public health crises abroad. Steve Ellis, President of Taxpayers for Common Sense joins Balance of Power to discuss the supplemental funding request.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Israeli forces shoot and kill Palestinians in occupied West Bank, Gaza
stories.summary: The deaths come amid escalating Israeli military raids and settler attacks across the occupied territories.

stories.headline: Iran tells ships in Strait of Hormuz to turn back
stories.summary: At least four vessels change course as Tehran challenges International Maritime Organization’s evacuation route

stories.headline: Trump’s Iran deal is better than ongoing war
stories.summary: The president has made many errors in his policy towards Tehran, but signing the MOU isn’t one of them

stories.headline: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power Over Immigration With TPS and Southern Border Decisions
stories.summary: The court handed President Trump victories in his push to rescind deportation protections for hundreds of thousands of people and turn away migrants at the southern border.

stories.headline: Migrants not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border, Supreme Court rules
stories.summary: The 6-3 decision clears the way for the Trump administration to resume allowing federal agents at the border to turn back asylum seekers before they enter.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court paves way for government to block asylum seekers at border
stories.summary: Liberal justices say the ruling 'circumvents' US law by allowing agents to prevent asylum seekers from filing a claim.

stories.headline: IRGC warns against new Hormuz route for ships: What we know
stories.summary: Experts say disagreements over shipping routes and transit fees could complicate efforts to reach a permanent agreement.

stories.headline: Poland's Tusk calls for 'mutual respect' during row with Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.headline: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
stories.summary: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

stories.headline: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
stories.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

stories.headline: What Are the Paths Forward for the Housing Bill After Trump Refused to Sign It?
stories.summary: President Trump abruptly scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill. It could still become law, with or without a presidential signature. But Mr. Trump could also try to kill it.

stories.headline: Kenya arrests more than 350 as people mark anniversary of deadly protests
stories.summary: Demonstrators mark the second anniversary of a 2024 protest where 60 people were killed by security forces.

stories.headline: Iran accuses NATO of ‘complicity’ in US war: What role did EU nations play?
stories.summary: NATO boss tries to convince Trump that European allies were there for the US during war on Iran.

stories.headline: France takes nuclear reactors offline amid record heatwave
stories.summary: EDF cites environmental rules protecting river ecosystems as rising water temperatures force output cuts at sites on the Seine and Rhone.

stories.headline: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
stories.summary: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

stories.headline: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
stories.summary: A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint.

stories.headline: After Trump’s Outburst, Senate G.O.P. Reverses Course on Iran
stories.summary: In a late-night vote aimed at mollifying the president, Senate Republicans rejected a resolution directing him to end the war against Iran, a day after a bipartisan rebuke.

stories.headline: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term

stories.headline: US Supreme Court scales back Roundup cancer lawsuits in victory for company
stories.summary: More than 100,000 plaintiffs had filed cases in US state and federal courts alleging cancer link to weedkiller Roundup.

stories.headline: Beaten and starved: Shock in India as police rescue men from bonded labour
stories.summary: Bonded labour was outlawed in India in 1975, but it still continues in villages and small towns.

stories.headline: Dimon promotes two potential successors at JPMorgan

stories.headline: Endless Shrimp Deal Was Scheme to Squeeze Red Lobster, Suit Says
stories.summary: Red Lobster’s $20 all-you-can-eat shrimp deal wasn’t just a badly designed promotion that inadvertently helped tip the iconic American restaurant chain into bankruptcy.

stories.headline: SpaceX Sale Shows Funding Across Equity, Credit: Hjort
stories.summary: Viktor Hjort, global head of credit strategy & desks analysts at BNP Paribas, and Stephanie Doyle, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Memory is a Structural Story for AI: Lovell
stories.summary: Nadia Lovell, Head of Global Equity Strategy at UBS Global Wealth, says memory is a stickier, structural story for AI and has longer legs to go within the trade. She speaks to Bloomberg's Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Stocks Drift as Apple Price Hikes Offset Chipmaker Strength
stories.summary: Stocks meandered through a choppy trading session that saw Apple Inc.’s move to raise prices on its products unwinding some of the enthusiasm over strong chipmaker forecasts.

stories.headline: Copper Climbs as Dip-Buying and AI Rally Offset Rate Concerns
stories.summary: Copper rallied from a seven-week low as signs of consumer buying and a rebound in global stock markets bolstered sentiment.

stories.summary: Claims health of staff and children at risk as France struggles to adapt heat-trap school buildingsEurope live – latest updatesTeachers in France are risking their own and students’ health in overheated schools as a severe heatwave sets new record temperatures, education unions said, urging staff to strike over “unacceptable working conditions”.Several teaching unions on Thursday issued a joint statement denouncing a “blatant lack of preparation”

stories.headline: Bedouins protest Israel’s home demolition policy in the Negev desert
stories.summary: Bedouins protest Israel’s home demolition policy in the Negev desert

stories.summary: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

stories.headline: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.headline: White House seeks additional $88 billion from Congress for Iran war, other priorities
stories.summary: The request mostly includes money for the Pentagon, with $67 billion going toward replenishing the military’s stocks of munitions and the cost of sending so many forces to the Middle East.

stories.headline: The 4 GOP senators who broke ranks and voted to block Trump from resuming Iran war
stories.summary: The president seethed at Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul after the war powers resolution passed, saying: “I will get it done, one way or the other.”

stories.headline: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
stories.summary: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election integrity bill. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

stories.headline: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
stories.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

stories.headline: Stocks Whipsaw as Micron Surges While Apple Sinks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A renewed wave of tech volatility gripped Wall Street, with a selloff in megacaps tempering optimism in other corners of the stock market that were buoyed by signs the economy is in good shape.

stories.headline: Oil Gains as Ship Attack Raises Concern About Hormuz Reopening
stories.summary: Oil rose as traders closely monitored shipments through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel was struck by a projectile in the key shipping corridor off the Omani coast, renewing concerns about the safety for passing cargoes amid the US and Iran’s peace negotiations.

stories.headline: Strange new EV-makers keep appearing in China
stories.summary: If you can make a robo-vacuum, you can make a car

stories.headline: Silicon Valley has much to learn from the spreadsheet jockeys it despises
stories.summary: Even if financial markets disagree

stories.headline: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

stories.headline: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
stories.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

stories.headline: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
stories.summary: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

stories.headline: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.headline: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.headline: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
stories.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.headline: US-Iran talks postponed as Israel launches deadly strikes in Lebanon
